nodes.context: A 'Ndrangheta-based organized crime family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and other crimes.
A Mafia family in Hamilton, Ontario, rivals of the Papalia family, involved in ordering hits on Papalia leaders.
One of three centralized Mafia organizations in Hamilton, Ontario, alongside Papalia and Musitano families.
An American Mafia family based in Buffalo, New York, with strong connections to the Papalia family.
A Mafia family from which Maria Rosa Italiano came, involved in the Perri-Starkman group.
A Montreal-based Mafia family that lent money to Papalia lieutenant Enio Mora.
An outlaw motorcycle club that gained Papalia associate Gerald Ward after Johnny Papalia's death.

edges.context: The Musitanos ordered the murder of Johnny Papalia and his lieutenant, leading to the decline of the Papalia family.
The Papalia and Luppino families are two of three centralized Mafia organizations in Hamilton.
The Papalias had strong connections with the Buffalo family, and Johnny Papalia was a made man in the Buffalo family.
Maria Rosa Italiano, from the Italiano clan, married Antonio Papalia, linking the families.
Papalia lieutenant Enio Mora borrowed $7.2 million from the Rizzuto family, leading to Mora's murder when not repaid.
After Johnny Papalia's death, associate Gerald Ward left the Papalias to join the Hells Angels.
